subject: Product Management
title: The Four Risks
description: A quick win: understand The Four Risks.

hook.text: Every product idea carries four risks. Discovery's job is to reduce all four before you invest heavily.

buildup.text: Value risk: will customers use it? Usability risk: can they figure it out? Feasibility risk: can we build it?

discovery.text: The fourth risk is viability: does it work for our business — legally, financially, and strategically?

twist.text: Most teams only check feasibility. Value and viability risks silently kill products after launch.

climax.text: Good discovery systematically attacks all four risks with the cheapest possible tests.

punchline.text: Four risks. Test all four. Before you build.

quiz.question: What are the four product risks discovery should address?
